meta.title: U10 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW X2 U10 engine — B38, B48, B47. Real UK costs, reliability data and common failures. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X2 U10 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned U10 engine typically costs £2,800–£7,500 supply only, depending on engine — the B38 three-cylinder sits at £2,800–£4,500, while the B48 M35i runs £4,500–£7,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW X2 U10 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B47 diesel (sDrive18d/xDrive20d) — the B-series engines are a significant reliability improvement over earlier N-series units, with the timing chain relocated to the front of the engine. The B48 petrol is a close second. Both are proven and durable.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X2 U10?

acceptedAnswer.text: B38/B48 petrol carbon build-up on intake valves, B47 diesel EGR cooler and DPF issues, B48 cooling system leaks (water pump, thermostat housing), and — with the new 48V mild-hybrid system — potential battery and electric motor issues as the system ages.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X2 U10 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the B-series engines (B38, B48, B47) are well-established across the BMW range and are widely regarded as reliable. The U10 is a new generation, so long-term data is limited, but early indications from the closely related U11 X1 suggest strong reliability. The 48V mild-hybrid system adds complexity but is generally robust.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW X2 U10 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — all are solid choices. However, the M35i's high-output B48 puts more stress on components, so it requires more diligent maintenance. The three-cylinder B38 is the most economical but can feel strained under load in the heavy U10. Choose based on your driving needs.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my U10 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Rough idle, loss of power, engine warning lights, coolant loss (B48), or persistent DPF warnings (diesel) are the key warning signs. For the 48V system, watch for reduced stop-start functionality or battery warnings. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X2 U10 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well‑kept £30,000 U10, a £4,000 B47 recon is ~13% of the car's value — safe. As these cars age and values drop, our 70% BMW threshold will apply — value the car first. For now, replacement is financially viable on most examples.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW X2 U10 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B47 diesel and B48 petrol can exceed 200,000 miles with proper servicing — the B-series engines are among BMW's most durable. The B38 three-cylinder is similarly robust if carbon build-up is managed with regular longer runs. The 48V battery typically has a lifespan of 5–8 years.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the U10 and the previous F39 X2?

acceptedAnswer.text: The U10 (2023–present) is the second-generation X2, built on the FAAR platform with a more dramatic fastback roofline, 48V mild-hybrid technology across all engines, and a revised interior. The F39 (2017–2023) is the first generation with older N-series engines (N20, N47) and a more conventional SUV shape. The U10 is a significant step forward in technology and refinement — see the F39 Generation Page for that model's specifics.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X2 U10 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on B38/B48/B47 units. Tell us your variant (sDrive16i, sDrive18i, sDrive20i, sDrive25i, xDrive25i, M35i xDrive, sDrive18d, xDrive20d) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: BMW X2 U10 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the second-generation X2 U10 — from the 1.5-litre B38 three-cylinder to the 2.0-litre B48 four-cylinder that powers the M35i, with 48V mild-hybrid tech across the range. Real UK replacement costs, honest failure data, and the powertrain changes that split this generation's petrol and diesel line-up. Backed by 256+ real UK U10 enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW X2 U10 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the second-generation X2 U10 — from the entry-level three-cylinder petrol to the range-topping M Performance four-cylinder, all featuring 48V mild-hybrid technology.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: All reliability ratings are [PROVISIONAL] — no dedicated Engine Page exists yet for any U10 engine family. However, the B-series engines (B38, B48, B47) are well-established across the BMW range and are widely regarded as a significant reliability improvement over earlier N-series engines. The U10 is a new generation, so long-term data is limited, but early indications from the closely related U11 X1 suggest strong reliability. Ratings are derived from cross-referencing specialist and owner sources.

overview.intro: The U10 is the second generation of the BMW X2, unveiled in October 2023 and positioned as a sportier, coupé-like alternative to the X1. Built on the FAAR front-wheel-drive architecture shared with the U11 X1 and MINI Countryman, the U10 adopts a more dramatic fastback roofline, following the design language of the larger X4 and X6. The engine range is exclusively four-cylinder (and three-cylinder) units from BMW's B-series modular family, all featuring 48V mild-hybrid technology for improved efficiency and reduced turbo lag. A fully electric iX2 variant is also offered alongside the combustion-engine models.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW FAAR (front-wheel-drive architecture, shared with U11 X1) • Predecessor: BMW X2 F39 (2017–2023) • Successor: Currently in production (no successor announced) • Sibling body styles in this generation: None (X2 is a standalone SAC)
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The U10 X2 generated 256 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], concentrated on the B38 petrol and B47 diesel units as these compact SUVs enter the used market and owners seek reliable replacement options.

items.engine: B47 C20 B (2.0-litre diesel, sDrive18d / xDrive20d)
items.quote: The B47 is BMW's modular four-cylinder diesel that replaced the notorious N47, with the timing chain relocated to the front of the engine for improved durability. Delivering 150–163 PS and strong torque (360 Nm), it offers excellent fuel economy (50+ MPG) with 48V mild-hybrid assistance for smoother stop-start and reduced turbo lag. It's the most efficient engine in the U10 range.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who need diesel economy and torque for long-distance driving.

items.engine: B48 A20 P (2.0-litre petrol, sDrive25i / xDrive25i)
items.quote: The 2.0-litre B48 delivers 204 PS and 300 Nm of torque with smooth, refined power delivery. It's BMW's modular four-cylinder petrol engine, sharing the B-series family's improved reliability over earlier turbocharged BMW engines. With 48V mild-hybrid assistance, it's responsive and efficient. It's the pick for buyers who don't need M35i performance but want more punch than the three-cylinder.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want strong petrol performance without the M35i's running costs.

items.quote: While the B38 is generally reliable, the three-cylinder layout can feel strained under load in the heavy U10. Carbon build-up on intake valves is a known issue with direct-injection engines, particularly with urban driving. Early examples may also experience coolant system issues as the engine ages. However, these are manageable risks rather than catastrophic failures.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the most affordable U10 and accept the three-cylinder's character.

items.engine: B48 A20 H (2.0-litre petrol, M35i xDrive)
items.quote: The M35i's high-output B48 produces 300 PS and 400 Nm of torque. While the B48 is a robust engine, the increased power output places additional stress on the turbocharger, fuel system, and cooling system. A full engine replacement can exceed £7,500 — the most expensive U10 engine to repair.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want M Performance power and are prepared for higher maintenance costs.

items.quote: The 170 PS three-cylinder delivers surprising punch with 48V mild-hybrid assistance, returning genuine 40+ MPG economy. It's the entry point to the U10 range but punches above its weight — smooth, characterful, and the most affordable to run. With a 7-speed automatic gearbox, it's perfectly adequate for daily driving.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the lowest running costs and don't need four-cylinder performance.

items.quote: The xDrive20d is often overlooked in favour of the petrol models, but it's the ultimate U10 for long-distance touring — 163 PS, 360 Nm of torque, all-wheel drive, and genuine 50+ MPG economy. It's the U10 for buyers who actually drive their cars.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the most efficient U10 with all-weather capability.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Solid Choice — three-cylinder, cheap to run, proven B38

rows.commonMajorFailure: Oil filter housing leaks, coolant system
rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Petrol — refined, powerful, reliable B48

rows.commonMajorFailure: Turbo wear, fuel system, cooling system
rows.ownershipVerdict: Most Expensive — high-output B48, more stress on components

rows.commonMajorFailure: EGR cooler, DPF (urban use), timing chain (rare)
rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Diesel — proven B47, front-mounted chain, excellent economy

economicsRule.title: The U10 rule
economicsRule.text: A £3,200–£5,500 B47 recon against a £25,000–£45,000 U10 sits at 7–22% of the car's value — comfortably within the 70% BMW threshold. The U10 is a new generation, so replacement costs are a smaller proportion of the vehicle's value compared to older models. However, as these cars age and values depreciate, the same calculation will apply — value the car first.

keyTakeaways.question: Which U10 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: sDrive20i (B38) — three-cylinder petrol, cheap parts, no DPF, best fuel economy in the petrol range.

keyTakeaways.question: Which U10 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M35i xDrive (B48 A20 H) — high-performance B48, more stress on components, higher fuel consumption.

keyTakeaways.question: Which U10 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: sDrive25i (B48 A20 P) — four-cylinder petrol performance without the M35i's running costs, strong residuals.

problems.title: B38 / B48 Petrol Carbon Build-up
problems.affectedModels: U10 sDrive16i, sDrive18i, sDrive20i (B38 A15 P), sDrive25i, xDrive25i (B48 A20 P), M35i xDrive (B48 A20 H), 2023–present.
problems.rootCause: Direct-injection engines like the B38 and B48 are prone to carbon build-up on intake valves. The lack of fuel washing over the intake valves (as in port-injection engines) allows carbon deposits from the PCV system to accumulate. Short journeys and urban driving make the problem worse. Symptoms include rough idle, reduced power, and increased fuel consumption.
problems.recommendation: Use premium fuel and take the car on regular longer runs to help burn off carbon deposits. A £250–£500 professional cleaning is far cheaper than the £2,800+ replacement that follows neglected carbon build-up. Consider an oil catch can to reduce PCV oil entering the intake.

cta.label: Compare B38 engine prices for your U10 →

problems.title: B47 Diesel EGR Cooler & DPF Issues
problems.rootCause: The B47 diesel's EGR cooler can develop leaks or become clogged with soot, leading to coolant loss, poor running, and engine warning lights. The DPF (diesel particulate filter) can clog with repeated short journeys, requiring forced regeneration or replacement. BMW has issued recalls on some B47 vehicles for EGR cooler issues.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of reduced performance or an engine warning light, have the EGR and DPF systems inspected. A £350–£700 service is dramatically cheaper than the £800+ replacement that follows a cracked cooler or blocked DPF. Regular long motorway runs help prevent DPF issues.

cta.label: Compare B47 diesel engine prices for your U10 →

problems.title: B48 Cooling System Leaks (All Variants)
problems.affectedModels: U10 sDrive25i, xDrive25i (B48 A20 P), M35i xDrive (B48 A20 H), 2023–present.
problems.rootCause: The B48 petrol engine is known for cooling system issues — the water pump, thermostat housing, and oil filter housing can develop coolant leaks. The plastic coolant pipes can become brittle and crack, especially at the connection points. This is a well-documented BMW B-series engine weakness.
problems.recommendation: Check coolant levels regularly and inspect for any signs of leaks around the water pump and oil filter housing. A £250–£550 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £3,500+ replacement that follows an overheat.

cta.label: Compare B48 engine prices for your U10 →

problems.title: U10 48V Mild-Hybrid Battery & System Issues
problems.rootCause: The 48V mild-hybrid system adds complexity to the powertrain — the battery pack, electric motor (integrated into the 7-speed DCT), and associated electronics can fail. Symptoms include reduced stop-start functionality, reduced power assistance, and warning lights.
problems.recommendation: Have the 48V system checked during routine servicing — a £200–£400 diagnostic check is dramatically cheaper than the £1,200+ replacement that follows if the battery or motor fails. The 48V battery typically has a lifespan of 5–8 years.

cta.label: Compare U10 48V system diagnosis and repair quotes →
